Pub/sub is architectural through Zenoh declare_publisher/declare_subscriber for inter-daemon/node data and tokio::sync::broadcast for dataflow-finished notifications, plus per-subscriber log/topic streams. Oneshot channels are common for request/reply and lifecycle acknowledgements, including daemon/coordinator replies, dynamic-node requests, WebSocket shutdown, operator init, and process-finished signals. True MPMC competing-consumer work queues were not a dominant pattern; flume is MPMC-capable and used in several internal queues, but observed production uses mostly have a single active receiver per queue rather than a worker pool.
